Be Jeff Green:

• Sold $162M of $TTD at ~$120 in Jan 2025
• Stock later collapsed ~80%
• Just bought $148M at ~$25

Sold ~5x higher than he’s buying it back now.

SOFTWARE'S NOT DEAD

• $OKTA +10%

THE BATTLE OF FRONTIER AI MODELS

OpenAI
• Annualized revenue: ~$25B 
• Valuation: ~$840B
• Cash burn: ~$115B cumulative through 2029
• Weekly users: 800M
• Traffic share: Fell from 87% to 65% in 12 months

Anthropic
• Annualized revenue: ~$19B 
• Valuation: ~$380B
• Cash burn: ~$3B annually
• Claude Code ARR: $2.5B
• Enterprise: 80% of revenue (subscriptions 4x since January)

One is winning the consumer race (for now) while the other is winning enterprise AI.

Berkshire Hathaway’s incoming CEO Greg Abel just disclosed a $15M purchase of $BRK.B shares.

The company also confirmed it has resumed stock buybacks for the first time since 2024.
$NVDA has halted production of China-bound H200 chips and is shifting $TSM capacity to its next-gen Vera Rubin platform.

$AMZN launched Amazon Connect Health, an AI platform that automates scheduling, verification, documentation & coding while integrating with healthcare EHR systems.

The system can handle up to 80% of call tasks with early users reporting 30–60% lower call abandonment.
Cathie Wood bought ~$15M of $AMZN today

$HOOD unveils the Platinum Card (made with actual platinum) offering higher limits, lounge access & premium perks.

$695 annual fee includes 5% cash back on dining & flights, 10% on hotels, ~$800 in travel credits & memberships like $AMZN Medical & Oura.

$RGTI reported $1.9M of Q4 revenue and is valued at ~$6B.

For context, $GOOGL generated ~$17M in profit per hour during the same quarter.

$AVGO transformation has been incredible. 

• Five years ago: ~$25B revenue with ~$5B profit
• Today: ~$68B revenue with ~$28B profit

They now make more in gross profit than they generated in total revenue five years ago.

$AVGO says hyperscalers will stick with direct-attach copper as long as possible given its lower latency, power & cost versus optical.

That extends the copper era which is a major tailwind for $CRDO whose AEC cables expand copper’s reach inside large AI clusters.

OpenAI reportedly held early talks with $TTD about helping automate and scale advertising within ChatGPT.

The company believes ads could help double consumer ChatGPT revenue to ~$17B this year.

*CIRCUIT BREAKER TRIGGERED AFTER S. KOREA'S KOSPI PLUNGES 8%
*KOREA'S KOSPI EXTENDS DROP TO 10% AFTER TRADING RESUMES

The chart shows that the South Korean KOSPI stock index has doubled since last August (green) and has already corrected 17% from Friday's high (red), including a 10% correction today.

This market is not for the faint of heart.

South Korea's market is retail-dominated. It is estimated that up to 70% of its trading is retail, compared to about 20% for the NYSE.

For years, South Korea was the world's largest equity options market by contract volume, even larger than the United States. This is a remarkable fact for a mid-sized economy.

This is what retail-dominated markets do. They don't rally, they double. They don't correct, they crash.

South Korea imports 94% of its oil, with 75% coming from the Middle East. So, it is easy to see why its "degens" are panicking.

The US is reportedly considering capping $NVDA H200 sales in China to 75,000 per customer, which is said to be less than half of what companies like Alibaba $BABA and ByteDance are looking to purchase, per Bloomberg.

$AMD

Nebius $NBIS has received approval to construct its flagship data center in Missouri scaling up to 1.2GW, with reports suggesting the facility will cost $6.6 billion to construct with first operations expected by 2028.

$CRWV $NVDA

Meta $META has signed a $50 million per year AI licensing agreement with News Corp, parent of WSJ, MarketWatch, IBD and Barron’s, with the deal set to last for at least three years and allowing Meta to use content in the US and UK.

Optical interconnect startup Ayar Labs raised $500 million in a Series E round valuing it at $3.75 billion, with participation from Nvidia $NVDA, AMD $AMD, MediaTek, and others. 

Ayar Labs says the capital will help accelerate production and test capacity of its co-packaged optics solutions.

📺 THE MARKET’S BIGGEST TRAP RIGHT NOW

It has been one of the most difficult and volatile trading weeks in a long time. Despite geopolitical headlines and sharp overnight moves, every attempted breakdown quickly failed.

Key levels like $SPY $675–$682 and $QQQ around $593–$599 were reclaimed, pulling the market right back into the range. 

That tells you the market is not ready for a sustained downside move yet, even though volatility is high.

So, don’t force big directional bets right now in this range-bound market. React to levels, not headlines or predictions you hear on TV or Fintwit. Stay balanced and trade selectively – only stocks showing relative strength.

This remains a trader’s market, not a trend investor’s market, where risk management matters the most.

Godzilla didn't destroy Tokyo.

The carry trade might.

$20 trillion.

Zero rates.

20 years.

Global leverage nobody talks about. 

August 2024 was a preview. 

The $VIX hit 65.

I made this to explain why.

And the conclusion is critical.

Few.

Bitcoin at $73,000.

Down 42% from its high. Equities bleeding. Gold being sold for margin calls.

And BTC is... holding?

The "digital gold" narrative failed for a year. Now actual gold is getting liquidated and Bitcoin decides to work.

The irony writes itself.

Broadcom AI revenue: $8.4 billion.

Up 106% year-over-year.

Q2 guide: $22 billion in revenue. Up 47% YoY.

$10 billion buyback announced.

War in the Middle East. Recession fears everywhere.

AI doesn't care. AI keeps spending.

S&P 600 up 9% YTD.

S&P 500 flat.

Tech lagging. 

Energy leading. 

Value crushing growth.

This isn't a head fake. 

This is a regime change.

The small-cap rotation is real and it's just getting started.

The most dangerous phrase in markets right now:

"It's already priced in."

Bitcoin at $70,774.

Up 3.5% while equities bleed. Gold crashes. Bonds do nothing.

The "digital gold" narrative failed for a year.

Now, when actual gold is being sold to meet margin calls, BTC decides to work?

The irony is not lost.

Few.

Lockheed Martin up 6% Monday.

Down 1.3% today.

The defense trade isn't fading because the war is ending.

It's fading because the market is starting to realize war doesn't fix earnings multiples.
